version_format_policy.unknown_version_behavior: fail_closed, not_evaluable, or explicit human disposition according to the applicable RFC
version_format_policy.admission_boundary: implementation support for a version does not admit that version for use

classification_policy.classification_basis: semantic effect, not textual diff size
invariants: large textual diff != semantic break by itself
implementation supports version != version admitted for use

must_prove_all: no previously valid object identity changes meaning
no authority verb changes meaning or owner
no previously conformant implementation becomes silently nonconformant without a declared profile boundary
old behavior remains valid within an explicitly versioned compatibility mode
downgrade and negotiation rules prevent authority widening
existing conformance claims are accurately scoped and are not represented as successor-version conformance
compatibility_preservation_profile_excep.failure_behavior: if any required proof is absent, resulting_version_increment is MAJOR
compatibility_preservation_profile_excep.non_inference_rule: a MINOR version number does not imply that a Compatibility Preservation Profile exists or is accepted

change_class_records.migration_requirement: none unless a non-semantic artifact relocation requires an informative pointer update
change_class_records.claim_invalidation_behavior: must not invalidate a conformance claim because no normative meaning changed
change_class_records.requirement_id_behavior: preserve the existing requirement ID and version history
review_requirements: verify that normative meaning is unchanged
verify that no conformance obligation or public claim boundary changed
record the semantic change class separately from the resulting version increment
change_class_records.ecn_interaction: an ECN cannot be normalized into an editorial change
forbidden_misclassification_cases: any change to object or state meaning
any change to public claim boundaries

change_class_records.normative_definition: Makes an existing normative meaning more explicit without expanding or narrowing permitted behavior.

resulting_version_increment.condition: the review proves that no requirement semantics or conformance impact changed

change_class_records.conformance_impact: none; any changed conformance outcome is not clarifying
change_class_records.migration_requirement: none unless the clarification reveals a contradiction that requires a separately classified change
change_class_records.claim_invalidation_behavior: must not invalidate or widen a claim because the permitted behavior is unchanged
change_class_records.requirement_id_behavior: may preserve the requirement ID only when the meaning remains unchanged
change_class_records.successor_rfc_behavior: no successor RFC required when the meaning remains unchanged
review_requirements: evidence that no requirement semantics changed
explicit review of every forbidden semantic effect
record the semantic change class separately from the resulting version increment
change_class_records.ecn_interaction: an ECN restriction cannot be recast as a clarification
examples_informative_only: making an already required condition explicit without expanding or narrowing behavior
resolving ambiguous wording while preserving the sole previously valid meaning

change_class_records.normative_definition: Adds optional behavior, extension points, or stricter bounded support without breaking existing conformant implementations.

resulting_version_increment.condition: existing conformant implementations remain conformant and no mandatory unknown semantic becomes permissive

change_class_records.conformance_impact: existing conformant implementations remain conformant; new behavior is separately scoped
change_class_records.migration_requirement: declare negotiation, extension namespace, and optional-adoption behavior
change_class_records.claim_invalidation_behavior: existing claims remain scoped to their exact version and must not be represented as successor-version conformance without evaluation
change_class_records.requirement_id_behavior: preserve unchanged requirement IDs; assign a new primary requirement ID to each new independently testable normative obligation
change_class_records.successor_rfc_behavior: not required unless the additive semantics belong to a separately owned RFC
review_requirements: record the semantic change class separately from the resulting version increment
change_class_records.ecn_interaction: an ECN cannot introduce additive behavior or widen use
examples_informative_only: stricter bounded support that leaves existing conformant behavior valid
forbidden_misclassification_cases: existing conformant behavior becomes silently nonconformant

change_class_records.normative_definition: Narrows previously permitted behavior to preserve safety or sovereign invariants.

resulting_version_increment.conditional_alternative_gate: an accepted Compatibility Preservation Profile with a stable reference proves every required bounded-compatibility condition

change_class_records.conformance_impact: previously conformant behavior may become nonconformant and must not be silently invalidated
change_class_records.migration_requirement: required, including existing-claim invalidation consequences and any compatibility mode expiry
change_class_records.claim_invalidation_behavior: declare which existing claims remain valid within their original exact version and which are restricted or invalidated
change_class_records.requirement_id_behavior: preserve an ID only where the obligation remains semantically compatible; otherwise create a successor requirement or major RFC revision
change_class_records.successor_rfc_behavior: a successor RFC is permitted when a major in-place revision would obscure identity or ownership
review_requirements: Compatibility Preservation Profile proof when MINOR is proposed
record the semantic change class separately from the resulting version increment
change_class_records.ecn_interaction: an ECN may temporarily restrict or suspend use but cannot become permanent text without a normal RFC revision and disposition
examples_informative_only: narrowing previously permitted behavior to preserve a safety invariant
forbidden_misclassification_cases: MINOR inferred solely from the phrase restrictive compatible
MINOR inferred from a version number
Compatibility Preservation Profile absent, unaccepted, or unbound
previously conformant implementation silently becomes nonconformant
existing claims are represented as successor-version conformance without evaluation

change_class_records.normative_definition: Incompatibly changes object identity, Authority semantics, state meaning, proof requirements, required fields, interface behavior, failure behavior, or conformance obligations.

resulting_version_increment.allowed_alternative_condition: explicit predecessor and successor identity, requirement mapping, migration consequences, and human disposition

change_class_records.conformance_impact: prior conformance does not imply conformance to the breaking revision or successor
change_class_records.claim_invalidation_behavior: existing claims remain bounded to their original exact RFC version, subject, environment, restrictions, and validity interval
change_class_records.requirement_id_behavior: create an explicit successor requirement or major RFC revision; never silently reuse an ID for materially incompatible semantics
change_class_records.successor_rfc_behavior: permitted with exact predecessor and successor mappings and preserved history
review_requirements: record the semantic change class separately from the resulting version increment
change_class_records.ecn_interaction: an ECN cannot substitute for permanent breaking-change disposition
examples_informative_only: Authority owner or verb meaning changes
required proof or failure behavior changes incompatibly
forbidden_misclassification_cases: classified as editorial because the textual diff is small
classified as clarifying while evidence or conformance semantics change
classified as additive while a prior conformant implementation breaks
history rewritten without predecessor and successor mappings

migration_and_claim_rules: deprecation, supersession, restrictive revision, breaking revision, ECN issuance, and requirement withdrawal must declare effects on implementations, profiles, schemas, tests, deployment admissions, conformance claims, and public claim language
conformance to one RFC version must not be represented as conformance to a successor without evaluation against the exact implementation and environment
a migration window preserves a historical claim only within its original RFC version, evaluated subject, environment, restrictions, and validity interval
a migration window does not imply conformance to a successor RFC
